incidents.rcaDescription: The billing batch job failed because the expected input file was not present in the designated directory. This occurred due to a failure in the upstream file transfer process.
incidents.resolutionSummary: Upload billing file and rerun job
resolutionSteps: Check if the upstream file generation job completed successfully
Verify file transfer logs for any failures
Manually upload the billing file to the correct directory
Verify file naming convention matches expected pattern
Re-run the billing batch job

incidents.rcaDescription: The payroll processing job failed to establish a connection to the database server. Investigation revealed that the database listener service was not running, preventing any connection attempts.
resolutionSteps: Check database listener status using 'lsnrctl status'
Review database alert logs for any errors
Restart the database listener service
Verify database connectivity using tnsping
Re-run the payroll processing job

incidents.rcaDescription: The customer report generation job failed with an OutOfMemoryError. The JVM heap size was insufficient to handle the large dataset being processed, causing the application to crash.
resolutionSteps: Review current JVM memory settings (-Xmx parameter)
Analyze heap dump to identify memory consumption patterns
Increase JVM heap size to at least 4GB (-Xmx4g)
Consider implementing data pagination for large reports
Re-run the report generation job with updated settings

incidents.rcaDescription: The billing batch job failed because the service account does not have permission to access the billing data directory. Recent security policy changes removed read access for the batch job service account.
resolutionSteps: Verify file permissions on the billing directory using 'ls -la'
Check if the service account has read access
Review recent permission changes in the system
Grant necessary read permissions: chmod 755 /data/billing
Re-run the billing job after permission fix

incidents.rootCause: Database server overload during peak hours
incidents.rcaDescription: The ETL sales job failed due to a database connection timeout. The target database was experiencing high load during peak hours, causing connection attempts to exceed the configured timeout threshold.
incidents.resolutionSummary: Increased connection timeout settings and optimized database queries
resolutionSteps: Verify database server status and current load
Check database connection pool settings
Increase connection timeout from 30s to 120s
Review and optimize slow-running queries
Consider scheduling job during off-peak hours
Re-run the ETL job after configuration changes

incidents.rootCause: Source system changed file format without notification
incidents.rcaDescription: The inventory sync job failed because the input file format changed from CSV to pipe-delimited format. The source system updated their export process without notifying the downstream teams.
incidents.resolutionSummary: Update file parser to handle new format
resolutionSteps: Analyze the new file format and delimiter
Update the file parser configuration to handle pipe-delimited files
Add format validation logic to detect future changes
Coordinate with source system team for change notifications
Test with sample file before production deployment
Re-run the inventory sync job
incidents.logExample: ParseException: Expected 10 columns but found 1 at line 2

incidents.rootCause: Log files filled up disk storage
incidents.rcaDescription: The order export job failed because the server disk reached 100% capacity. The application was unable to write output files due to insufficient storage space.
incidents.resolutionSummary: Free disk space and clean old logs
resolutionSteps: Check disk usage using df -h
Identify large files using du -sh
Delete or archive old log files
Configure automatic log rotation
Re-run the order export job
incidents.logExample: java.io.IOException: No space left on device

incidents.rcaDescription: The payment reconciliation job failed because the application server could not reach the database host due to a temporary network routing issue.
resolutionSteps: Ping the database server to verify connectivity
Check network routing configuration
Verify firewall rules
Restart network services if required
Re-run the reconciliation job

incidents.rootCause: Dependent job did not complete successfully
incidents.rcaDescription: The order export job failed because its upstream dependency job did not complete successfully, preventing the scheduler from triggering the job execution.
incidents.resolutionSummary: Fix upstream job and restart scheduler workflow
resolutionSteps: Check upstream job execution logs
Resolve failure in dependency job
Restart the scheduler workflow
Verify dependency chain configuration
Re-run the order export job
incidents.logExample: SchedulerException: Job dependency failed for ORDER_EXPORT_JOB
